mission.title: Install and Configure Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE)
mission.description: This mission guides you through the installation of Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE), a managed Kubernetes service on Oracle Cloud. It simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters, allowing you to focus on your applications rather than the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on Oracle's cloud infrastructure and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the OCI CLI
steps.description:
The OCI CLI is required to interact with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. This step installs the CLI on your local machine.

```bash

curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cli/master/scripts/install/install.sh | bash

```

> This command downloads and installs the OCI CLI.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster in Oracle Cloud. Specify the desired version, node count, and region.

```bash

oci ce cluster create --name my-k8s-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.30.2 --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..your_compartment_id --vcn-id ocid1.vcn.oc1..your_vcn_id --node-pool-ids '[

```
commands: oci ce cluster create --name my-k8s-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.30.2 --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..your_compartment_id --vcn-id ocid1.vcn.oc1..your_vcn_id --node-pool-ids '[

steps.title: Configure kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step sets up your kubeconfig file to interact with your new Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --file $HOME/.kube/config --region us-phoenix-1 --token-version 1.0

```

> This command generates a kubeconfig file for your cluster.
commands: oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --file $HOME/.kube/config --region us-phoenix-1 --token-version 1.0

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure additional features like autoscaling, monitoring, and RBAC for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cloud-controller-manager/main/deploy/oci-cloud-controller-manager.yaml

```

> This command deploys the OCI Cloud Controller Manager.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

oci ce cluster delete --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --force --wait-for-state TERMINATED

```

> This command will remove the cluster and its resources.
commands: oci ce cluster delete --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --force --wait-for-state TERMINATED

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
After deleting the cluster, check for and manually delete any orphaned resources like load balancers and PVCs.

```bash

oci lb load-balancer list --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..your_compartment_id

```

> List load balancers to identify any that need to be deleted.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This command upgrades the cluster to the specified version.

```bash

oci ce cluster update --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --kubernetes-version 1.30.2 --wait-for-state ACTIVE

```

> This command upgrades the cluster to the specified version.
commands: oci ce cluster update --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --kubernetes-version 1.30.2 --wait-for-state ACTIVE

upgrade.title: Verify Upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the nodes after the upgrade to ensure they are functioning correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Ensure all nodes are in 'Ready' status after the upgrade.

upgrade.title: Rollback if Necessary
upgrade.description:
If issues arise after the upgrade, you may need to rollback to the previous version. Ensure you have a backup before proceeding.

```bash

oci ce cluster update --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --kubernetes-version 1.29.2 --wait-for-state ACTIVE

```

> This command rolls back the cluster to the previous version.
commands: oci ce cluster update --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --kubernetes-version 1.29.2 --wait-for-state ACTIVE

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized' when running OCI CLI commands.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your OCI CLI is not configured correctly or your credentials are invalid.

**Fix:**
Reconfigure the OCI CLI with valid credentials using 'oci setup config'.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl get nodes returns 'No resources found'.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate that the cluster is not properly configured or the kubeconfig is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig file and ensure it points to the correct cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Load balancer not created for service of type LoadBalancer.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The OCI Cloud Controller Manager may not be deployed or configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the OCI Cloud Controller Manager is deployed and check its logs for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Node status shows 'NotReady'.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate issues with the node's connectivity or configuration.

**Fix:**
Check the node's logs and ensure it has proper network access to the Oracle Cloud.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new OCI features, improved load balancer management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Removed deprecated APIs related to node management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.29, ensure to review the new load balancer configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the nodes listed with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --file $HOME/.kube/config --region us-phoenix-1 --token-version 1.0

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
